 {"id":"bd-u4c1","title":"Replace hardcoded SVG band/axis colors with CSS custom properties","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded hex color strings in `render_layout_band()` and `render_layout_axis_tick()` functions in fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs with CSS custom property references.

## Why
These functions render gantt section labels (#4a5568), axis tick marks (#94a3b8), and axis tick labels (#64748b). These colors are theme-unaware — they are legible on the Default light theme but invisible on Dark, Neon, and Blueprint themes where the background is dark.

## Technical Context
These two functions (`render_layout_band` at line ~2050 and `render_layout_axis_tick` at line ~2104) only receive `&SvgRenderConfig` — they do NOT have access to the `Theme` struct. The options are:
1. Thread `&ThemeColors` through the function signatures (invasive refactor)
2. Use CSS custom properties like `var(--fm-text-color, #fallback)` (minimally invasive)

Option 2 is preferred because the theme CSS already defines these properties via `ThemeColors::to_css_vars()` in theme.rs, and SVGs with embedded `<style>` blocks will correctly resolve them.

## Specific Changes
- `render_layout_band()` line ~2095: Change `.fill("#4a5568")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #4a5568)")`
- `render_layout_axis_tick()` line ~2112: Change `.stroke("#94a3b8")` to `.stroke("var(--fm-edge-color, #94a3b8)")`
- `render_layout_axis_tick()` line ~2124: Change `.fill("#64748b")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #64748b)")`

## Status
PARTIALLY DONE — The CSS var approach was applied in the latest session. Verify the changes are committed and golden tests blessed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Band labels use `var(--fm-text-color)` with appropriate fallback
- Axis ticks use `var(--fm-edge-color)` for stroke and `var(--fm-text-color)` for text
- All 10 theme presets produce readable axis labels in gantt/timeline diagrams
- Golden SVG snapshots blessed after change","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:40.755243683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:00.042010891Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:00.041388102Z","close_reason":"Applied CSS var approach for band/axis colors in latest session","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-u785","title":"EPIC: Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness","description":"## Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness

Build an automated visual regression testing harness that renders the complete FrankenMermaid test corpus to SVG/PNG, compares against golden reference images using perceptual diff, and produces a browsable HTML report. This serves dual purpose: regression detection AND an impressive demo of FrankenMermaid's rendering capabilities.

## Motivation
Layout algorithms are notoriously difficult to regression-test with unit tests alone. A pixel-level change in coordinate assignment can propagate to produce visually different (but structurally correct) layouts. Conversely, a bug might produce structurally incorrect output that looks "close enough" to pass manual review. The regression harness provides:
1. **Automated comparison:** perceptual diff between current and golden renders
2. **Browsable report:** HTML gallery showing before/after/diff for each test case
3. **Demo showcase:** the report itself IS the demo — browse all diagram types, see FrankenMermaid's quality
4. **Regression budget:** configure acceptable diff threshold (e.g., 0.1% pixel difference) per diagram type

## Perceptual Diff Algorithm
Use structural similarity index (SSIM) or perceptual hash (pHash) rather than pixel-exact comparison:
- **SSIM:** Compares luminance, contrast, and structure. Score from 0 to 1, where 1 = identical.
  SSIM(x,y) = [l(x,y)]^α * [c(x,y)]^β * [s(x,y)]^γ
  where l = luminance, c = contrast, s = structure comparison
- **pHash:** DCT-based perceptual hash. Hamming distance between hashes measures visual similarity.
- **Threshold:** SSIM > 0.99 = no regression, 0.95-0.99 = warning, < 0.95 = regression

## Implementation
1. Create `regression-harness/` binary crate in workspace.
2. Input: directory of .mmd files (test corpus) + golden reference renders.
3. Pipeline per file: parse → layout → render to SVG → rasterize to PNG (via resvg or similar).
4. Compare each PNG against golden reference using SSIM.
5. Generate HTML report: thumbnail grid, click to expand, before/after/diff overlay.
6. Golden reference update: `cargo run --bin regression-harness -- --update-goldens`.
7. CI integration: run on every PR that touches fm-layout or fm-render, fail if any SSIM < threshold.
8. Include timing data per render in the report (performance regression detection).

## Demo Aspect
The regression report doubles as the project's visual showcase:
- Categories: flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, gantt charts, etc.
- Each category shows 5-10 representative diagrams at various complexities.
- Report includes metadata: node count, edge count, layout time, render time.
- Hosted version can be published to frankenmermaid.com/gallery for public demo.

## Success Metrics
- All diagram types in test corpus rendered and compared automatically
- SSIM threshold tuned to catch real regressions while ignoring sub-pixel jitter
- HTML report loads in < 2 seconds with 500+ thumbnails
- Golden update workflow is one command + explicit commit
- CI gate blocks merge on regression (SSIM < 0.95)
- Report generation completes in < 5 minutes for full corpus

## Dependencies
- Requires fm-render-svg (bd-1y5) for SVG output
- Requires test corpus (bd-2xl.1 golden test infrastructure)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:39.374613334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:18.947294342Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","layout","regression","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:18.947218691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:44.324472620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:44.427880609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Strip comments before extracting body and add tests for leading comment braces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T22:12:43.451672330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T22:34:30.175872382Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T22:34:30.175673084Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ulag","title":"Implement diagram-level title in IR and render across all diagram types","description":"# Diagram-Level Title Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
Many diagram types support a title directive:
\`\`\`
---
title: My Diagram Title
---
flowchart LR
  A --> B
\`\`\`
Or inline: \`title My Diagram\` in gantt, journey, timeline, pie, xychart, quadrant.

The title renders as centered text above the entire diagram.

## Current State
- YAML front-matter title: NOT extracted from init config
- Inline 'title' directive: Parsed and SKIPPED in gantt, journey, pie, etc.
- MermaidDiagramMeta has NO title field
- SVG renderer does NOT render any diagram title

## Implementation Plan
1. **IR** (fm-core): Add title: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **Parser**: In front-matter parsing, extract title field. In diagram-specific parsers (gantt, journey, timeline, pie, xychart, quadrant), capture the title directive text and store in meta.title.
3. **SVG Renderer**: When meta.title is Some, render as centered <text> element above the diagram content. Adjust viewBox to accommodate title height.
4. **Tests**: YAML front-matter title, inline title in gantt, inline title in pie, no title (verify no empty text element), long title (verify wrapping or truncation).

## Impact
This is a high-visibility feature — users expect their diagram title to show up. Currently it silently vanishes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:52.006771142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:33:17.573322578Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T05:33:17.572874430Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-ulag","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-uwtl","title":"Add classDef styling diagnostic: emit warning when classDef is parsed but ignored","description":"## Problem (interim fix before full classDef wiring)
Until bd-65l0 (full classDef→SVG wiring) is complete, users who write classDef directives get ZERO feedback that their styles are being silently dropped. This interim bead adds a diagnostic warning so users at least KNOW their styles aren't working.

## Scope
1. In fm-parser (or fm-core validation), after parsing classDef directives, emit a Warning-level diagnostic:
   "classDef directive parsed but not yet applied to rendered output. Styling will use theme defaults."
2. Same for `style` directives on individual nodes.
3. Make the diagnostic machine-readable (category: Compatibility, rule_id: "classdef-not-applied").

## Why This Matters
Silent failure is the worst UX. Even "unsupported" is better than silently dropping user intent. This is a 30-minute fix that dramatically improves the user experience until full classDef support ships.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `fm-cli validate` on input with classDef shows a warning about unsupported styling.
- `fm-cli render` on input with classDef includes the diagnostic in verbose output.
- Test: input with classDef produces at least one Compatibility warning.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:20.344430316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:39:54.687067495Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:39:54.686886322Z","close_reason":"Added rule_id 'style-directive-not-applied' to existing compatibility warning for classDef/style/linkStyle directives. Updated test to verify rule_id.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uwtl","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:04.870271228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vb9","title":"EPIC: Advanced Layout Algorithms","description":"Upgrade fm-layout with superior layout algorithms that produce significantly better-looking diagrams than mermaid-js. The existing Sugiyama implementation is solid but we need: (1) dramatically improved cycle handling that doesn't just reverse edges but produces aesthetically pleasing layouts, (2) force-directed layout for organic/non-hierarchical diagrams, (3) tree layout for mindmaps/org charts, (4) specialized layouts for timeline/gantt/sankey/kanban/grid diagrams, (5) constraint-based refinement for fine-tuning. The layout engine is the single biggest factor in diagram quality -- this is where we win against mermaid-js. All algorithms must maintain the determinism guarantee: identical input always produces identical output.

## Success Criteria

- Every capability listed in this epic is delivered completely through child issues; no scope cuts and no loss of planned functionality.
- Child tasks include comprehensive unit/integration coverage and e2e coverage with detailed logging; test evidence is available and reproducible.
- Performance, determinism, and reliability expectations for this epic are validated with measurable checks.
- User-facing behavior is documented (README/docs/config/migration notes) for all newly shipped functionality.
- Release readiness is proven by passing workspace quality gates and epic-level validation flows end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:28.678967346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:45.549811122Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:45.549421174Z","close_reason":"All 8 children closed: superior cycle handling (3 strategies + cluster collapse), force-directed layout, tree/radial algorithms, specialized layouts (timeline/gantt/sankey/kanban/grid), crossing minimization (barycenter + sifting), orthogonal edge routing (self-loops, parallel edges), automatic algorithm selection, time-budget guardrails with fallback. 10 layout algorithms total. 197 layout tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb9","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-vb9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Idea-wizard expansion: added bd-vb9.8 (layout time-budget guardrails + deterministic fallback) to protect reliability on pathological graphs.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:27Z"}]}
